reviews.review_description: Outstanding piece of FOSS, and a fantastic contribution to the Gaming on Linux Community  in general. My sincere respect to all its developers. Thank you for leaving this to the world to enjoy.
lowest latency option so far
the people who make wivrn are lovely and have made an amazing piece of software i cannot recommend it enough for anyone using linux vr
Works really well. I've had no issues.

reviews.review_title: Love this app, great for Linux streaming
reviews.review_description:
I’m able to stream VR games from my Linux devices and most run without issues.
Thanks to the developer adding new features this app is a must have for a Linux user!

Thank you!

I was struggling trying to get ALVR to work properly for me, literally just for an initial connection and then I stumbled across this software.

Its connection is seamless, just be on the same WIFI and the desktop and Quest discover each other fine.

The connection quality is great (though probably depending on your specific internet. I would assume if you have crappy internet it won’t be as great).

And no features are missing. On my computer I can just launch a VR game, for example Phasmophobia, and it connects perfectly.

Overall an exceptional FREE software that makes Linux life easier.
Might be on me, but trying to get this running in federal on amd hardware is miserable

reviews.review_title: it does the work for you.
reviews.review_description: its super easy. took me 2 mins after failing other softwares

reviews.review_title: Best app to pair with Steam Deck
reviews.review_description: This app is just what you need if you have a Steam Deck and don't want to deal with SteamVR running through ALVR. Remember to set the resolution to 50% before you launch any XR app on it.

reviews.review_title: Every game I try has some issue
reviews.review_description: I can get games to load, they look good, and run smoothly but there's always a game breaking bug such as dlc not working or being in the floor with no way to center the view.  Another game the controls didn't work. 😩
Works just fine with my Fedora Linux install.

reviews.review_title: It is awesome for vr gaming on Linux
reviews.review_description: When i am developing vr software, it is a massive time saver to use this as opposed to uploading the apk to the headset. Love using this app.
Simply the best option for Linux users, especially those trying to avoid SteamVR and its many bugs.

Thank you guys so much for putting this out there! I've found it to be a far more comfortable experience than ALVR, and that it works better with my machine (cachyOS, AMD). 
I'll make sure to donate once I can, especially since this is an open source project. 
Now to ditch Windows completely.
SteamVR Linux comes from the deepest pits of software hell and im so glad i dont have to use it anymore
It works almost flawlessly, can't complain about a thing!
Works great but needs a way to recenter and for dlc to work I use it with my steam deck and it works almost perfectly apart for those cases

reviews.review_title: Just what I needed for VR on Linux
reviews.review_description: Works great playing Contractors VR under Proton-GE, no longer any noticeable stuttering which I used to get while playing using Air Link on Windows.

reviews.review_title: Working great with Linux Desktop PC
reviews.review_description:
Working my way through Half Life: Alyx with this!

For HL:A specifically envision is a nice project too - makes it straightforward to use an OpenConnect branch that supports Alyx ('alyx' branch of OpenMoonclaw's OpenOVR tree).

reviews.review_title: Saved my half life alyx gaming experienc
reviews.review_description: Envision + WiVRn + OpenComposite ftw
Very user-friendly and a great place to start.
